keywords: self-tapping screws, drill screws, tek screws, metal screws, construction fasteners, roofing screws

body.en: Self-tapping screws (also called self-drilling or Tek screws) have a sharp point and specially designed thread that allows them to create their own mating thread in materials like sheet metal, plastic, or wood. Unlike standard screws that require pre-drilled pilot holes, self-tapping screws drill their own hole as they are driven in, significantly speeding up installation. Related resources: high-tensile bolts anchor bolts stainless steel fasteners hex bolts lock nuts washers self-drilling screws socket screws. This procurement framework applies to fastener sourcing for construction, mining, energy, automotive, and general industrial applications. The decision logic prioritizes quality consistency, supplier reliability, and total cost of ownership over short-term unit price. For B2B buyers in Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia, additional considerations include regional customs requirements, container consolidation options, and payment security mechanisms. Standardized specifications that travel across projects reduce training time, RFQ processing time, and supplier onboarding effort. There are three main types: Type AB point screws have a Type A point with a finer thread, suitable for thin sheet metal up to 0.9mm. Type B point screws have a 45-degree Type B point, ideal for thicker materials up to 2.3mm. Drill point (Type III) screws have a flat tip with drill flutes, capable of drilling through steel up to 5mm without pre-drilling.  Standards-based procurement requires continuous learning as ISO, ASTM, DIN, and GB standards are revised every 3-5 years. Subscribe to standards update notifications and review specifications annually. The most common standards used in international fastener trade are ISO 898-1 (mechanical properties), ISO 3506-1 (stainless), ISO 3269 (acceptance inspection), ISO 4042 (coatings), and ISO 1502 (thread gauges). For B2B buyers, building a standards reference library with at least the top 20 standards relevant to your product mix is essential. Self-tapping screws are essential in African construction: roofing installation—attaching metal roofing sheets to purlins; steel framing—connecting steel studs and tracks; HVAC ductwork—securing duct sections; solar panel mounting—fixing panel frames to racking systems; and general sheet metal fabrication for commercial buildings. The comparison methodology above uses typical ranges from industry data and TradeGo procurement experience 2018-2024. Actual market prices fluctuate with raw material costs (steel, zinc, nickel), energy costs, labor costs, and currency exchange rates. For accurate comparison, request identical RFQs from 3-5 qualified suppliers and compare landed cost (product + shipping + insurance + duties). For African environments, coating selection is critical. Zinc-plated screws offer basic protection for indoor use. Black phosphate screws provide temporary protection and lubrication during installation. For outdoor and coastal applications, stainless steel (A2 304 or A4 316) or hot-dip galvanized screws are essential to prevent rusting and premature failure.  The verification checklist applies to both initial supplier qualification and ongoing batch inspection. For high-risk applications (load-bearing structural, pressure vessels, mining, marine), add fatigue testing, salt-spray testing (ASTM B117), torque-tension testing, and post-installation inspection. The cost of additional verification is 0.1-0.3% of order value, while undetected quality failures can be 10-100x the order value in damages. heading.en: How to Select the Right Self-Tapping Screw

body.en: Selection criteria: Material being fastened—determine thickness and type (steel, aluminum, wood). Load requirements—heavier loads require larger diameter and more threads. Environmental exposure—coastal and outdoor applications need corrosion-resistant coatings. Installation speed—drill point screws offer fastest installation through steel. Use this formula: for steel up to 2.3mm, use Type B; for steel 2.3-5mm, use Type III drill point.  Supplier evaluation should be a continuous process, not a one-time exercise. Re-evaluate suppliers annually based on delivery performance, quality metrics, price competitiveness, and responsiveness. Maintain a supplier scorecard with weighted metrics: on-time delivery (25%), quality acceptance rate (30%), price competitiveness (20%), responsiveness (15%), and documentation accuracy (10%). Drop suppliers scoring below 70% over a 6-month period and qualify replacements.  Supplier evaluation should be a continuous process, not a one-time exercise.
